prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿提踵" (single-leg calf raise) exercise, side view, facing right, with the right foot/ankle clearly visible at the bottom of the frame. Person posture: Athletic person at the TOP PEAK of a single-leg calf raise on the RIGHT leg. The right foot is balanced on the BALL OF THE FOOT (toes only) with the right HEEL MAXIMALLY LIFTED OFF THE GROUND — there is a CLEAR VISIBLE GAP between the right heel and the floor, ankle in full plantarflexion. The right leg is straight, supporting the entire body weight. The LEFT leg is lifted off the ground with the left knee bent at 90 degrees and the left foot tucked behind the right ankle (left foot does NOT touch the ground). Torso upright with neutral spine. Left hand placed lightly on a wall to the left for balance. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the right heel is visibly LIFTED HIGH OFF THE GROUND (balanced on toes), and only the RIGHT FOOT touches anything (left foot is off the ground entirely).. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) of the supporting (right) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: tibialis posterior and intrinsic foot muscles for stabilization. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腓肠肌" pointing to the gastrocnemius (upper calf); "比目鱼肌" pointing to the soleus (deep calf); "足部稳定肌" pointing to the foot stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
